tickets.title: §21.7 — Coexistence: build the grouped serving surface (two lenses over one hyperschema)
tickets.body:
IMPLEMENTATION-READY (design landed 2026-07-16, #114 — §21.7 in spec/21-schema-identity.md; implement to it, don't re-litigate). Build coexistence: two lenses (resolution Schemas) served over one hyperschema, per-lens at every door, with the degenerate single-lens case BYTE-IDENTICAL.

THE SHAPE (pinned by §21.7 — dedup is a data structure, not a discipline): derive ONE grouped serving surface — programs: Map<hyperschemaName, { hyperschema, roots: UNION of member bindings' roots, lenses: Map<lensName, binding> }> — computed in ONE place from the surviving bindings; every consumer iterates the groups, none sees the flat Bound[] list. Consumers: SchemaRegistry.build takes one hyperschema per group; the reactor registers ONE materialization per program over the union roots; GraphQL builds one family per LENS (types <LensName>View/<LensName>Patch, query/subscription field, mutation family, hooks dispatch keyed on lens name); REST's path segment is the lens; loam.public admits lens by lens; the §17 version ladder runs per (registration entity, lens).

THE GROUPING KEY is read from the binding's own bytes: lens name = the `schema` pointer's target id minus the `schema:` prefix (in the bytes since slice 2prime). Latest-wins narrows to latest-PER-LENS — registering FilmClassic no longer evicts Film. A pre-coexistence store regroups conservatively (one lens name per hyperschema = one group = today's reading).

REFUSALS AT THE SEAM: (a) two bindings naming one hyperschema with DIFFERENT bodies (termHash mismatch) are refused at grouping, loudly, before any state changes; (b) two lens names legalizing to the same GraphQL identifier are already refused by the existing collision checks (gql.ts:336) — verify the refusal now guards the lens namespace. REBIND RULE: a new lens whose roots are a SUBSET of its program's union rides the additive path; one that WIDENS the union forces a generation rebind (the reactor has no deregister). Decide by comparing unions at the seam.

NO §20 MIGRATION — §21.7's on-wire verdict is nothing breaks (no delta grows/loses a role, no entity id renamed); the register input's one non-default is schema.name naming the lens, defaulting to hyperschema.name (today's behavior, forever the degenerate case).

RAILS THE TICKET DEMANDS: byte-identical single-lens SDL/OpenAPI/introspection/REST paths (snapshot-compare against main); latest-per-lens (register two lenses, both serve, each evolves alone); the grouping refusals (same-name-different-body; same-legalized-name); the rebind rule (subset-rooted additive, widening rebinds); per-lens §17 versions; per-lens loam.public (publishing Film reveals nothing of FilmClassic — uniform 404); writability honesty (a field writable through one sibling lens is writable — §14's posture, assert it as BEHAVIOR, not as a bypass-guard).

CODE ANCHORS: registration.ts:661 (the latest-wins take), registration.ts:556-564 (survivingCandidates grouping), gateway.ts:525-619 (fixpoint + additive path + rebind — the flat-list consumption sites), gateway.ts:1447-1449 (matName), gql.ts:309-311/334-340 (name keying + collision refusal), src/surface/rest.ts:49-73.

VILLAGE: an act where one hyperschema carries two living lenses — the broad public reading and a narrow archival one — served simultaneously, evolving on their own clocks, one publicly declared and one not.

FUTURE (not this ticket): VersionedHyperSchema (Film@termHash — build when a pin needs the gather frozen).

MERGE: no capability/auth/federation/erasure surface and no migration, but it rewires the gateway's core serving loop — if the classifier hesitates, it is Myk's merge (P6).

tickets.title: §27 — Membership is a query, first-class: select/watch + composable set-algebra scoping
tickets.body:
BUILD-READY. The first §27 implementation slice (design DECIDED and landed — #110/#112; the 0.6.0 substrate landed — #113): make a container's membership a first-class delta-query, exactly §27.6's decided shape (a rhizomatic Term → dset, static or live, local or remote).

DO: (1) Expose the evaluation as a gateway surface — select(term): evaluate a JSON op-profile Term (parseTerm → evalTerm, dset-sort required) over this store's ground, once; watch(term): the same, live (re-evaluated on pulse — a parameterized offeredDeltas, which spec/27.6 calls 'nearly free'). Refuse a non-dset Term loudly at the door. (2) Generalize the quarantine's seeding edge: QuarantineOptions gains a membership Term alternative to the admit predicate (admit stays as the degenerate case, per §24.10 — same knob, generalized, no new mechanism); the inbound federation filters the pulse by the Term. (3) Prove the composable scoping 0.6.0 bought: a pool seeded over a difference ('author X's deltas MINUS container Y's members'), including a NESTED difference, live-follows correctly — the exact composition the depth-1 select(not(inView(...))) idiom could never express.

BOUNDARY (0.6.0, pinned in §24.10 — do not fight it): difference/intersect are Term-layer operators ONLY; they may NOT ride inside inView predicates (depth-1 stratification unchanged). Compose scopes at the seeding/reading EDGE; invent no predicate-level machinery, no parallel vocabulary.

DO NOT BUILD (each its own later ticket): the module manifest (§27.6 question 1 — still OPEN, needs design); Merkle-set identity (§27.6 question 2); trust-on-load (§27.6 question 3); the full Container lifting with the five-knob vector (§27.7 — comes when a SECOND policy wants the object); scope-merge's container-entity exclusion claims (needs the container-as-entity model); promote-LAW (§24.4).

RAILS: select returns exactly the Term's dset over a real ground; watch follows a pulse; a membership-Term-seeded pool admits exactly the members (and its erasure fan-out still holds — reuse the §24.8 rail against a Term-seeded pool); the nested-difference scope evaluates through the seeding edge; a non-dset Term is refused.

CODE ANCHORS: quarantine-pool.ts (QuarantineOptions.admit — the degenerate form being generalized); test/spike/set-algebra.test.ts (the 0.6.0 operators' proven reach, reuse its idioms); gateway.federate + offeredDeltas (the pulse being filtered); UNRUNNABLE_KEYS at src/federation/translate.ts:105 (difference/intersect need NO entry — they are Term ops, recorded there; do not add one).

VILLAGE: an act where the operator stands up a trial pool over a hand-picked scope — one author's claims minus an excluded container — watches it live-follow, and drops it. MERGE: touches the federation/quarantine seeding surface → Myk's merge (P6), never self-merge.
